patches.summary: reminders create + reminders cancel now skip the userdata.write call when --dry-run is set; the prior code only decorated the response envelope after the write had already executed.
patches.reason: Greptile P1 round-3 on PR #595: flags.dryRun was checked AFTER c.Post(path, body), so the snooze (create) or unreminder (cancel) write had already fired by the time the dry-run flag was honored. Gated the userdata.write call on !flags.dryRun and synthesize a {dry_run, path, method, planned_body, would_send_write} preview when dry-run is set. The userdata.read call earlier in reminders create stays live because it is a read-shaped POST and lets the preview include the real messageIds the write would have used.
patches.validated_outcome: go build / go vet / 566 tests / 17 packages green; verify-skill clean.

patches.summary: Extend the empty-from exclusion in ListAwaitingReply to ALL modes, not just --external-only.
patches.reason: Greptile P1 round-3 on PR #595: history.list stubs (rows inserted by ApplyHistoryDelta before a messages.get backfill) have empty from/subject/snippet, so default-mode awaiting-reply surfaced a useless `<blank> awaits your reply` row. The prior PATCH suppressed these only under --external-only on the grounds that a missing-from row could not be classified as external vs internal; the default mode kept them. Greptile flagged that even in default mode the row is noise. Extended the suppression to all modes. The cost is the same documented transient false negative (one poll cycle until backfill); the savings is no garbage row in the default response. The existing TestListAwaitingReplyExcludesEmptyFromUnderExternalOnly test was updated to assert the unbounded-mode exclusion too.
patches.validated_outcome: go build / go vet / 566 tests / 17 packages green.

patches.summary: Recording the os.Stdout/os.Stderr swap in runSyncResourceQuiet as a known limitation deferred to a syncResource(io.Writer, ...) refactor across all 3 call sites.
patches.reason: Greptile P1 round-3 on PR #595 raised that swapping process-global os.Stdout/Stderr affects every goroutine in the process, not just the runSyncResourceQuiet caller. The existing mitigation (sync.Mutex serializing the swap; PATCH comment naming the constraint) bounds the blast radius to the autorefresh path which is single-goroutine today. The principled fix is to thread an io.Writer through syncResource(c, db, resource, sinceTS, full, maxPages, latestOnly, userParams) so output goes to a per-call sink. That touches 3 caller sites (channel_workflow, sync, sync_autorefresh) and ~15 fmt.Fprintf(os.Stdout, ...) emissions inside syncResource and its helpers; out of scope for this PR.

patches.summary: Strip the 32-byte SHA-256(host_key) prefix only when it matches the computed hash so older-Chrome cookies are not silently truncated.
patches.reason: The 32-byte plaintext prefix is added by Chrome's M120+ cookie host-binding feature; older Chrome versions write cookies without it. The unconditional strip discarded the first 32 chars of every cookie value, so the session token sent to accounts.superhuman.com was always wrong and RefreshFromChromeCookies never succeeded. We now verify the prefix matches sha256(host) before stripping (greptile P1).

patches.summary: Send Superhuman backend identity headers on authenticated backend calls.
patches.reason: The published CLI needs the browser-derived x-superhuman headers to match the private backend contract.

patches.summary: Replace the generated promoted messages shortcut with the real send, unsend, reminders, snippets, and scheduling workflow.
patches.reason: Superhuman's send path is a multi-step backend workflow that cannot be represented by the original generated messages shortcut alone.

patches.summary: Add local-store-backed folder, search, thread filter, participant, awaiting-reply, RFC822 lookup, watch, and bootstrap workflows.
patches.reason: Agent workflows need composable local mailbox operations with explicit freshness metadata and Gmail history deltas.
patches.validated_outcome: PII scrub gate and full module tests pass.

patches.summary: Add automatic refresh provenance and opt-in response envelopes.
patches.reason: Agents need to know whether command data is fresh, local, or live without parsing command-specific output.

patches.summary: Expose token refresh state, binary age, and 401 re-login hints.
patches.reason: Operators need actionable diagnostics when stored browser credentials are stale or revoked.

patches.summary: Back snippets with Superhuman's SNIPPET-labeled backend threads instead of a local JSON store.
patches.reason: Superhuman snippets are stored in the UI as backend threads with a SNIPPET label, so CLI snippets should sync with the user's Superhuman account and only emit a non-destructive migration hint for older local JSON snippets.

patches.summary: Replace session-specific emails, Google user IDs, draft IDs, and names with example placeholders.
patches.reason: The published library must not contain user-specific mailbox artifacts from the implementation session.
patches.validated_outcome: Known-token grep gate returns zero matches.

patches.summary: HTML-escape plain-text send bodies before the <div> wrap.
patches.reason:
renderBody wrapped raw plain-text bodies in
, so user-supplied `<`, `>`, `&` (including
patches.validated_outcome: TestRenderBody_PlainTextEscapesHTML covers angle brackets, script tags, ampersands, and HTML-mode pass-through.

patches.summary: Lock HTTP response cache directory to 0o700 and cache files to 0o600.
patches.reason: writeCache created the cache dir with 0o755 and files with 0o644, exposing full email thread bodies to any local user on shared hosts. Token store was already 0o600; this brings the cache into parity. Surfaced by greptile P2 on PR #578.

patches.summary: Rewrite reminders create/cancel to call /v3/userdata.write with the full reminder value shape recovered from a live snoozed thread.
patches.reason: The generator-emitted /reminders/create endpoint returns 400 "missing threadId" on every body variant against ingested threads. The Superhuman web client snoozes threads via /v3/userdata.write writing to users/<userId>/threads/<threadId>/reminder with value {clientCreatedAt, keepOnReply, messageIds, onDesktop, reminderId, source: "USER", threadId, triggerAt} (all required; recovered by reading a real reminder back via userdata.read). cancel writes null to the same path -- Superhuman's standard delete-via-null-write convention, mirrors drafts_discard. The CLI now does userdata.read first to discover the thread's messageIds, generates a UUID for reminderId, formats triggerAt as ISO-with-nanos UTC, and maps --condition {always,if-no-reply} to the keepOnReply bool. --reminder-id is retained as a deprecated alias of --thread-id for back-compat. Full discovery + before/after wire shapes in .manuscripts/20260515-165115/discovery/reminders-sniff-report.md.
patches.validated_outcome: Live API: reminders create + reminders cancel both return HTTP 200 with currentHistoryId advance against mvh@esperlabs.ai. Unit tests: TestRemindersCreate_BackendCallShape pins the full 8-field value shape + sorted messageIds + UUID reminderId; full ./internal/cli suite passes (289 tests).

patches.summary: Remove the artificial 1-hour minimum on send --remind-in.
patches.reason: buildSendReminder rejected any duration shorter than 1h with "send: --remind-in must be at least 1h", but the Superhuman web app accepts 1-minute reminders and the backend round-trips them fine. The floor was generator-emitted client-side fiction. Now only non-positive durations are rejected (the parse layer already does that; this branch is defense-in-depth).
patches.validated_outcome: TestBuildSendReminderValidation covers 5m success; TestSend_ReminderFlagErrors covers 0s rejection.

patches.summary: ApplyHistoryDelta now writes a minimal stub via upsertGmailMessageStubTx instead of the full upsert, so a previously-fetched complete row stays intact when the same message reappears in a messagesAdded record.
patches.reason: storedMessageFromHistory builds a StoredMessage from gmail.HistoryMessage, which only carries id/threadId/labelIds/snippet/historyId/internalDate. Routing that through upsertGmailMessageTx ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE overwrote from/to/cc/subject/body with empty strings every time a history poll fired for a known message (greptile P1 on PR #595). The new stub upsert inserts the row on first sight, then on conflict only refreshes label_ids/history_id/synced_at (always) and thread_id/snippet/internal_date (only when the incoming value is non-empty / non-zero). data column is preserved as-is from the original full fetch.
patches.validated_outcome: go build + go vet + go test ./... pass (566/17). Full upsert path unchanged for complete-message ingestion.

patches.summary: threads list local-filter path now maps --type draft to DRAFT and errors out for done/archived instead of silently returning unfiltered results.
patches.reason: localThreadTypeLabel returned the empty string for draft, done, and archived, so combining those with --label / --participants-file / --intersect-with-stdin dropped the type filter entirely (greptile P1 on PR #595). draft has a single positive label (DRAFT) the existing filter can express; done and archived need negative-label semantics (not-INBOX, not-INBOX/SENT/SPAM/TRASH) the positive-only label index cannot represent, so the local path now returns a clear error pointing the user at the Gmail-passthrough route that supports them natively.
patches.validated_outcome: go build + go vet + go test ./... pass (566/17). Existing intersect-with-stdin test updated to pass --type sent explicitly (it was depending on the silently-dropped default-draft behavior).
